Parallel Sets (Alluvial) Plot

PlotAlluvial.Rd

Creates a parallel sets (alluvial) chart - a clean alternative to SCP's alluvials that preserves your own color palettes and theme.

Supports two or more axes via the axes parameter. Passing three or more columns (e.g. axes = c("BraakStage", "ThalPhase", "CERAD")) produces a multi-axis diagram ideal for multiparametric comparisons.

Use donor.by to aggregate to the donor / patient level before computing counts - streams then show donor flows rather than cell flows. This is the recommended approach for neuropathology scores, clinical metadata, or any variable that is defined per-individual rather than per-cell.

When facet.by is supplied, one alluvial panel is drawn per level and assembled with patchwork. Plot margins are auto-expanded based on the longest axis label so text is never clipped.

Usage

PlotAlluvial(
  data,
  axes = NULL,
  group.by = NULL,
  split.by = NULL,
  donor.by = NULL,
  facet.by = NULL,
  ncol_facet = NULL,
  alpha = 0.75,
  axis_width = 0.1,
  label = TRUE,
  label_size = 4,
  label_nudge = 0.1,
  group_colors = NULL,
  split_colors = NULL,
  axes_colors = list(),
  output_dir = NULL,
  object_name = "",
  pdf_width = 7,
  pdf_height = 5.5
)

Arguments

data

A Seurat object or a plain data.frame/tibble containing the metadata columns.

axes

Character vector of 2 or more column names to use as axes, from left to right (e.g. c("BraakStage", "ThalPhase", "CERAD")). Stream color is determined by the first axis.

group.by

Character. Left-axis column (retained for backwards compatibility). Ignored when axes is supplied.

split.by

Character. Right-axis column (retained for backwards compatibility). Ignored when axes is supplied.

donor.by

Character or NULL. Metadata column identifying the donor / patient (e.g. "Donor.ID"). When supplied, metadata is first deduplicated to one row per donor so stream widths reflect donor counts rather than cell counts - the right choice for patient-level pathological scores (BraakStage, Thal phase, CERAD, etc.). Default NULL.

facet.by

Character or NULL. Metadata column to facet by - one alluvial panel per level (e.g. "Diagnosis"). Default NULL.

ncol_facet

Integer or NULL. Number of panel columns when facet.by is used. Auto-computed (max 3) if NULL.

alpha

Numeric. Stream transparency. Default 0.75.

axis_width

Numeric. Width of the axis rectangles. The stream width is 1.5 × axis_width. Default 0.1.

label

Logical. Annotate each axis segment with its level name. Default TRUE.

label_size

Numeric. Label font size. Default 4.

label_nudge

Numeric. Horizontal gap between axis bar and label. Default 0.1.

group_colors

Named character vector of colors for the first axis (streams + left axis). Auto-assigned if NULL.

split_colors

Named character vector of colors for the last axis. Auto-assigned if NULL.

axes_colors

Named list of color vectors, keyed by column name (e.g. list(BraakStage = BraakColors, ThalPhase = ThalColors)). Takes precedence over group_colors/split_colors.

output_dir

Directory to save a PDF. NULL = no save.

object_name

Label prefix for the output filename.

pdf_width, pdf_height

PDF dimensions per panel in inches. Default 7 x 5.5.

Value

A ggplot2 or patchwork object (invisibly).

On this page
